sanskrit:
दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्यजेत्।
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत्॥
transliteration:
duḥkham ity eva yat karma kāya-kleśa-bhayāt tyajet
sa kṛtvā rājasaṁ tyāgaṁ naiva tyāga-phalaṁ labhet
literal_meaning: One who abandons prescribed duty merely because it is unpleasant or from fear of bodily discomfort does not attain the fruit of true renunciation; such abandonment is rajasic.
contextual_meaning: Avoiding necessary tasks because they feel hard or scary is pseudo-renunciation—an impulsive escape driven by aversion, not wisdom. It leaves the psyche restless and the ego unchanged, so no inner freedom is gained.
psychological_state: Avoidant-attachment to comfort; low frustration tolerance; rationalized procrastination masking performance anxiety.
trigger_scenario: Hitting "snooze" instead of going to the gym, telling yourself "it’s too cold, I’ll start tomorrow."
anchor_text: Face necessary work; fleeing discomfort is not true renunciation.
